tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Life Jackets Born From Loss Save Three Teens in the Water
stories.summary: A mother's grief became a gift: free life jackets from her program rescued three teenagers from dangerous waters, eight years after her own son's drowning.

stories.headline: Community Rallies: Strangers Save 79-Year-Old From Eviction Over Her Yard
stories.summary: When 79-year-old Beverly Thomas faced jail over her overgrown yard, attorney Norburt Sanek and dozens of volunteers showed up at her door to help—for free.

stories.headline: New Zealand's Rarest Parrot Roars Back From the Brink
stories.summary: Five years after zero kākāriki karaka lived in the Hawdon valley, conservationists brought the critically endangered orange-fronted parakeet back from nothing.

stories.headline: Brothers Meet in Omaha: One Hits the Championship Home Run
stories.summary: Rare family triumph as sophomore Kyle Branch and his brother both reach the College World Series—culminating in Kyle's three-run shot to glory.

stories.headline: Nanoparticles Open New Door to Fighting Parkinson's and Brain Disease
stories.summary: Cornell researchers led by Azmain Alamgir cracked the code to smuggle therapeutic antibodies into cells, sparking hope for Parkinson's and brain disease.

stories.headline: Cardinals Fans Rise for Nolan Arenado's Emotional Homecoming
stories.summary: Nolan Arenado received a standing ovation returning to Busch Stadium in opposing colors after five seasons with the Cardinals—a moment of pure recognition.

stories.headline: Woman Builds a Wind Phone for People to Grieve and Heal
stories.summary: After losing both parents, a woman created a peaceful outdoor phone booth where mourners speak to loved ones and find solace.

stories.headline: Ocean Treasures Rise Again: 'Extinct' Sea Stars Thriving off California
stories.summary: Eighteen giant sunflower sea stars—each spanning over three feet—thrive off Northern California after a disease nearly wiped out the entire population.

stories.headline: $3,000 Bay Window Appears for Family With Two Autistic Children
stories.summary: KT Fairglass installed a $3,000 bay window for free at Gordon Passmore's home, transforming comfort for a family with two autistic children.

stories.headline: First Patient Receives Pioneering Stem Cell Treatment for Huntington's
stories.summary: UCI doctors implanted neural stem cells into the first Huntington's patient in a historic trial backed by a $12 million grant, offering real hope for this devastating disease.

stories.headline: After a Decade of Silence, Sea Stars Stage a Baby Boom
stories.summary: After a decade of death, Oregon's sea stars are having a baby boom, restoring hope for kelp forests and entire marine ecosystems.

stories.headline: Contractor's Heart Leads Him to a Stranger's Door
stories.summary: After seeing a news report about Mrs. Green's overgrown trees, a Virginia contractor brought his crew and fixed it—no bill, no strings attached.

stories.headline: Endangered Sea Turtles Return to Their Protected Philippine Shore
stories.summary: Conservation teams celebrate as marine turtles nest twice in 16 days on Carmen's coastline—proof that habitat restoration truly works.

stories.headline: Ocean Bacteria Become a Clean Power Plant
stories.summary: Bioprocess engineer Giulia Castro transformed marine bacteria into bio-photovoltaic cells that generate 227 mW per square meter while cleaning the air.

stories.headline: New HIV Vaccines Fortify Immune Cells' Most Powerful Weapons
stories.summary: UNC researchers demonstrated vaccines that strengthen T cells against HIV's weak spots, bringing the hope of a true cure closer.
